title: Identify What A Screen Symbol Represents By Selecting It
summary: This source-backed reference procedure describes using the OptiSweep/RMS screen to identify what a symbol represents by selecting it and reviewing the information shown. The training also notes that the PowerPoint may provide supporting explanation.
when_to_use: Use when a user needs to determine what a visible screen symbol represents and the interface provides additional information after the symbol is selected.
not_for: Do not use to infer symbol meanings that are not explicitly shown on the screen or in the referenced training material.
Do not use as a complete symbol dictionary; the source does not provide the full symbol set, exact screen, or all displayed fields.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Select the symbol and review the displayed information to determine what it represents.

safety_notes: Use only the informational behavior supported by the source: selecting a symbol to view what it represents.
Do not infer meanings beyond what the screen or referenced PowerPoint explicitly provides.
The source does not specify the exact screen, symbol set, or displayed fields.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the screen containing the symbols

access_required: Access to the relevant OptiSweep or RMS screen containing the symbol
system_preconditions: The symbol to be identified is visible on the screen
The interface supports selecting the symbol to display additional information

visual_references.description: Training frame near the cited transcript segment; retrieval text notes nearby discussion about symbol meaning and selecting interface elements.
Training frame showing a right-side information panel referenced as providing more information after selection.

steps.title: Locate the symbol to identify
steps.purpose: Identify the on-screen symbol that needs clarification before selecting it.
steps.instruction: Locate the symbol on the screen that you want to identify.
steps.expected_result: The target symbol is visually identified and ready to be selected.
steps.healthy_condition: The symbol is visible on the screen and can be selected.
steps.failure_condition: The symbol cannot be found, is not visible, or the source does not provide enough detail to confirm the correct screen or symbol set.

actions.label: Find symbol
actions.exact_action: Locate the symbol you want to identify on the screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: General interface area referenced in the training segment where selecting items reveals more information, including nearby mention of symbol meaning.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the symbol is not visible on the available screen.
Escalate if you cannot determine whether you are on the correct screen because the source does not specify the exact interface.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript discussion: selecting the symbol helps indicate what it represents, and the presenter references looking back on the PowerPoint.

steps.title: Select the symbol
steps.purpose: Trigger the interface behavior that reveals what the symbol represents.
steps.expected_result: The interface responds to the selection and displays information associated with the symbol.
steps.healthy_condition: Selecting the symbol causes additional identifying information to appear.
steps.failure_condition: Selecting the symbol does not display any identifying information or the interface response is unclear.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The training segment associated with the statement that selecting the symbol helps indicate what it represents.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if selecting the symbol does not produce any visible information.
Escalate if the interface behavior differs from the training description.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript discussion: selecting the symbol helps indicate what it represents, and the presenter references looking back on the PowerPoint.

steps.title: Review the displayed information
steps.purpose: Use the information shown after selection to determine what the symbol represents.
steps.instruction: Observe the displayed information to determine what the selected symbol represents.
steps.expected_result: The user can identify the meaning or represented object of the selected symbol from the displayed information.
steps.healthy_condition: The displayed information clearly indicates what the symbol represents.
steps.failure_condition: The displayed information is absent, ambiguous, or insufficient to determine the symbol meaning.

actions.label: Review displayed information
actions.exact_action: Observe the displayed information to determine what the selected symbol represents.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Right-side panel referenced in training as an area that gives more information after selection.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the displayed information does not explicitly identify the symbol.
Escalate if the interface shows information that is unclear and no supporting training material is available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript discussion: selecting the symbol helps indicate what it represents, and the presenter references looking back on the PowerPoint.

steps.title: Check the referenced PowerPoint if needed
steps.purpose: Use the training material mentioned in the source when the on-screen information alone is not sufficient.
steps.instruction: If needed, compare what you see to the referenced PowerPoint material mentioned in the training.
steps.expected_result: The PowerPoint provides supporting explanation that helps confirm what the symbol represents.
steps.healthy_condition: The PowerPoint is available and helps clarify the symbol meaning.
steps.failure_condition: The PowerPoint is unavailable or still does not clarify the symbol meaning.

actions.label: Consult training material
actions.exact_action: If needed, compare what you see to the referenced PowerPoint material mentioned in the training.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the symbol meaning remains unclear after checking the available training material.
Stop if no referenced PowerPoint is available and the screen does not explicitly identify the symbol.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript discussion: selecting the symbol helps indicate what it represents, and the presenter references looking back on the PowerPoint.

success_criteria: The selected symbol's meaning or represented object is identified from the displayed information.
If needed, the referenced PowerPoint confirms the interpretation.
healthy_conditions: Selecting the symbol displays identifying information.
The displayed information is sufficient to determine what the symbol represents.
failure_conditions: The source does not specify the exact screen, symbol set, or displayed fields.
The interface does not show enough information after selection to determine the symbol meaning.
The user attempts to infer meaning beyond what the screen or referenced training material explicitly provides.
escalation_guidance: Escalate for SME review if the symbol meaning is still unclear after selection and review of the referenced training material.
Escalate if the current interface does not behave as described in the training segment.
